steps.title: Use the central angle rule
steps.content: A central angle has its vertex at the center of the circle, and its degree measure equals the degree measure of the intercepted arc.

steps.title: Recognize the central angle
steps.content: Angle $\angle UMV$ has its vertex at $M$ (the center) with rays extending to points $U$ and $V$ on the circle. This is a central angle, and it directly intercepts arc $UV$.

steps.title: Use the central angle property
steps.content: The measure of a central angle equals the measure of its intercepted arc. Therefore, $\angle UMV = 110^{\circ}$.
